text: The question, When the Temple would be destroyed, is answered in the greater part, at least, of verses 4-35. The question, When should the world be brought to an end, is chiefly answered in verse 36.

Dan. 9.27. The Evangelist here gives a hint to the reader, probably indicating that the sign mentioned by Daniel had been recognizably fulfilled when he wrote. But there is no certainty now what event he alludes to.

Our Lord says that at the time when the false prophets arise, it will be easy to answer the question where the danger is coming from, just as it is easy for a man who sees dead carrion to prophesy where the birds will gather. ‘Eagles’ may be an allusion to the standards carried by the Roman armies.
Such words as these were often used, by a metaphor, of the fall of kingdoms or dynasties; Is. 13.10; Ez. 32.7; Jl. 2.10; 3.15.
‘The land’, or possibly ‘the earth’, but cf. Zach. 12.12.
‘Angels’; the Greek word used here might also mean ‘messengers’.
